description: Palo Alto PAN-OS threat ID ranges and syslog log types

events.description: Application detection signatures - identifies applications traversing the firewall regardless of port. Detect shadow IT, unauthorized apps (P2P, tunneling, remote access tools), and policy violations. App-ID is more reliable than port-based rules.
File blocking signatures - blocks file transfers matching configured types. Detect executable downloads, script file transfers, and potentially malicious file types crossing security boundaries.
Custom vulnerability signatures - user-defined IPS rules. Organization-specific protections for internal applications, custom protocols, or emerging threats not yet covered by vendor signatures.

Phone home detection signatures - detects outbound C2 communication patterns. Matches known C2 protocols, beaconing patterns, and malware callback behaviors. High-priority alerts requiring immediate investigation.
Spyware phone home signatures - detects spyware/adware calling home. Lower severity than C2 but indicates compromised endpoints. Correlate source IP with endpoint inventory for remediation.

Vulnerability protection signatures - IPS signatures matching CVE exploits. High severity hits require immediate investigation. Correlate with asset vulnerability data to determine if target is actually vulnerable.
File type identification signatures - identifies file types regardless of extension. Detects extension masquerading and facilitates file-type-based policies.
Data filtering signatures - DLP pattern matching. Detects credit card numbers, SSNs, and custom data patterns leaving the network. Investigate for data exfiltration attempts.

WildFire malware signatures - cloud-analyzed file verdicts. Indicates known malware matched by sandbox analysis. Files were detonated in WildFire cloud and confirmed malicious. Requires sample submission to be effective.

WildFire inline ML signatures - machine learning-based detection applied inline. Catches zero-day malware without cloud round-trip delay. Higher false-positive potential than signature-based detection.
DNS Security signatures - malicious domain detection. Blocks C2, DGA domains, DNS tunneling, newly registered domains, and phishing at the DNS layer. Essential for detecting encrypted C2 traffic.

events.event_name: Advanced Threat Prevention inline cloud-analyzed signatures
events.description: Advanced Threat Prevention inline cloud-analyzed signatures - real-time cloud analysis for advanced threats. Combines ML, behavioral analysis, and threat intelligence for zero-day detection with cloud-scale analysis.
Traffic log - connection start/end/deny with source/dest IP, port, zone, rule, bytes, session duration. Essential for network baseline, anomaly detection, and post-incident traffic reconstruction.
Threat log - virus, spyware, vulnerability, URL filtering, file blocking, data filtering events. Primary security event stream. Correlate threat-id with signature database for context.
System log - PAN-OS system events including admin logins, config changes, firmware updates, HA failover. Detect unauthorized management access and configuration tampering.
Configuration log - all configuration changes with before/after values. Essential for change management audit trail. Detect unauthorized rule modifications and policy weakening.
HIP Match log - GlobalProtect Host Information Profile results. Endpoint compliance check outcomes. Identify non-compliant devices connecting to VPN.
GlobalProtect log - VPN portal and gateway events. Monitor for VPN brute force, unusual connection sources, and concurrent sessions indicating credential sharing.
Authentication log - all authentication events. Monitor for brute force patterns, authentication from unusual locations, and MFA bypass attempts.
User-ID log - user-to-IP mapping events. Essential for identity-based policy enforcement. Stale mappings may cause policy misapplication.
Tunnel Inspection log - GRE and non-encrypted tunnel events. Detect unauthorized tunneling that may bypass security controls.
SCTP log - SCTP association events. Monitor for unauthorized SCTP usage which may indicate telecom-specific attacks or protocol abuse.
Decryption log - SSL/TLS decryption events. Monitor for decryption failures (certificate pinning bypass failures) and certificate issues that may indicate MITM or C2 using custom certificates.
Correlation log - events correlated by PAN-OS analytics engine. Pre-built detection logic combining multiple log sources. Higher confidence than individual events.
